description: According to Trendmicro, Royal ransomware was first observed in September 2022, and the threat actors behind it are believed to be seasoned cybercriminals who used to be part of Conti Team One.
name: royal

techniques.technique_details: A spearphishing email was sent to employees.
Qbot was launched through the Windows Command Shell with cmd.exe.
techniques.technique_name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Windows Command Shell

techniques.technique_details: Cobalt Strike was executed through encoded PowerShell commands.
Qbot DLL was added to HKCUEY_CURRENT_USER \Software \Microsoft \Windows \CurrentVersion \Run.
techniques.technique_name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder

techniques.technique_details: Cobalt Strike was installed as a Windows service on multiple systems.
techniques.technique_name: Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service

techniques.technique_details: Royal ransomware operators used (privileged) domain accounts for lateral movement.
Royal ransomware operations executed a known UAC bypass that abuses a default scheduled task to launch PowerShell with escalated privileges.
techniques.technique_name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Bypass User Account Control

techniques.technique_details: Password protected file containing an ISO file with a hidden file used in combination with an LNK file to execute Qbot.
techniques.technique_name: Obfuscated Files or Information: HTML Smuggling

techniques.technique_details: Royal ransomware operators used domain accounts for lateral movement.
Qbot and Cobalt Strike were both injected into legitimate Windows processes.
The FindLocalAdmin PowerSploit script was used to find local administrator accounts on workstations/servers.
Users and groups were enumerated with built-in Windows utilities and with AdFind software.
Domain trust was enumerated with built-in Windows utilities.
Network shares were enumerated with PowerSploit software.
Remote admin shares C$ were mounted from the Patient 0 workstation.
The Royal ransomware operators leveraged credential hashes from privileged accounts to perform lateral movement.
techniques.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Pass the Hash

techniques.technique_details: Several (privileged) domain accounts were used during the attack for lateral movement and deployment of ransomware.
Cobalt Strike uses peer-to-peer communication over Windows named pipes encapsulated in the SMB protocol.
Qbot and Cobalt Strike used HTTPS traffic for their C2 communication.
Royal ransomware operators used Mega Cloud Storage and Dropbox to exfiltrate data from multiple hosts.
techniques.technique_name: Exfiltration Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage

techniques.technique_details: Royal ransomware encrypted files on systems with the .royal extension.
